topic: North Pole cosmic mountain — c6 change-driven (post c5 patch dedup gaps)
change_summary: Two patches landed on WIN-031 since c5: (a) commit 9aaff53 (Decider integrate EXP-211/215/216) appended the c5-recommended scorecard-accounting sentence to detail_verdict_text without removing the pre-existing summary sentence, leaving two consecutive 'Nevertheless, WIN-031 is counted in the dome's...' sentences; (b) commit 6b81df2 (Decider self-apply: WIN-031 ISS-1361 completion + evidence double-paren polish) edited the 1 Enoch sentence in detail_evidence to address c5 minor hole #3 ('seven sacred mountains at the earth's center') but introduced a phrase duplication ('seven sacred mountains in a visionary cosmic geography' immediately after 'fills in a visionary cosmic geography'). detail_verdict_length grew from 692 to 1169 chars (+69%), tldr_evidence newly recorded.
our_argument_summary: WIN-031 remains classified Unfalsifiable. The c5 substantive arguments (retrodiction, non-discriminating archetype, scorecard-accounting category error) are all intact. Two prose-level bugs introduced by the c5-follow-up patches now require dedup/cleanup: a duplicated 'Nevertheless...' sentence pair in detail_verdict_text and a duplicated 'in a visionary cosmic geography' phrase in detail_evidence. Neither bug undermines the verdict, but both are visible to readers who expand the Verdict or Evidence panels and will be flagged by any careful dome defender as 'sloppy editing.' Severity: both major (visible-to-reader prose duplication is exactly the kind of artifact we criticize the dome model for).

EXP-216_followup_ISS-1362b_scorecard_acc.verification: The c5-recommended sentence ('Nevertheless, WIN-031 is counted in the dome's 69-confirmed / 94.5%-accuracy scorecard alongside empirically tested claims like Schumann frequency and SAA field strength — pricing theological convergence at par with data-grounded physics.') was successfully appended to detail_verdict_text — but the pre-existing summary sentence ('Nevertheless, WIN-031 is counted in the dome's confirmed-prediction scorecard alongside empirically testable WINs — the scorecard accuracy figure does not distinguish between testable predictions and unfalsifiable theological reframings.') was NOT removed. Both now sit consecutively, both starting with 'Nevertheless,' both saying functionally the same thing in different phrasings. Verified by node: detail_verdict_text matches /Nevertheless/g 2 times. The c5 hole #1 recommendation explicitly said 'Append one sentence... do not replace the whole field' — but the surrounding cleanup of the redundant generic version was not part of the patch instructions, so it slipped through.

ISS-1361_completion_double_paren_polish_.verification: Commit 6b81df2 attempted to address c5 minor hole #3 by softening 'seven sacred mountains at the earth's center' (which mis-locates 1 Enoch's seven mountains, which are 'beyond the waters to the west' in 1 Enoch 18:6-9, with the central throne-mountain in 24-25). The fix dropped 'at the earth's center' but the editing introduced a phrase duplication: 'fills in a visionary cosmic geography with seven sacred mountains in a visionary cosmic geography.' The phrase 'visionary cosmic geography' now appears twice in the same sentence — once as the verb-object, once as the prepositional rider for 'mountains.' Verified by node: detail_evidence matches /visionary cosmic geography/g 2 times. The fix replaces a factual nit with a worse stylistic bug — readers see broken prose.

c5_hole_1_detail_verdict_scorecard_accou.evidence: The accounting critique IS now in detail_verdict_text. But it was added as a duplicate of an already-existing sentence rather than replacing it. The critique reaches the reader; the prose quality is degraded.

c5_hole_2_zaphon_flat_earth_cartography_.evidence: The recommended preemptive clause ('This cartographic convergence is equally compatible with a spherical earth, on which north points toward a geographic pole, or with any other axially-symmetric geometry — so the texts' directional usage distinguishes no cosmology in particular.') was NOT added to argument (2). The current argument (2) still ends 'this is a consequence of geographic orientation, not dome geometry.' which a defender can flip as 'precisely — flat-earth cartographic orientation is the ancient witness.' c5 hole #2 was severity moderate; carry forward.

c5_hole_3_1_enoch_seven_mountains_locati.evidence: The 'at the earth's center' phrase was removed. Hole #3 was technically resolved — but the replacement introduced the new prose duplication captured above as patch_correct: false.

c5_hole_4_tldr_zaphon_zion_overgeneraliz.evidence: tldr_evidence still asserts all six texts are 'rooted in the Ancient Near East Mount Zaphon / Mount Zion tradition' — 1 Enoch 17–18 is Second Temple apocalyptic and inherits but is not strictly 'rooted in' the Zaphon-Zion idiom. c5 hole #4 was severity minor; recommendation was 'leave the TLDR as-is unless a reader specifically flags it' — defer to that disposition.

relevant_files: raw-text/02-wins.txt (WIN-031 listed CONFIRMED, no monitor criteria)
raw-text/05-model.txt ('North pole mountain (WIN-031)' in architecture paragraph)
data/sections.json part2b (monitor.py at commit 8e4bf9b with 39 domains)
dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: WIN-031 has no calculation pipeline. The 'CONFIRMED' label is a static string in the dome site. The pole's geometric role (r=0 in H(r), aetheric source in toroidal flow) is computed for other WINs (029, 038), not for WIN-031.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: 100% hardcoded/label-only. No API call, no measurement, no comparison.
data_sources_used: None — WIN-031 uses no empirical data source
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: No discrepancies. The 39-domain / 28-active / commit-8e4bf9b figures cited in detail_verdict_text are consistent with sections.json part2b prose and the analyst's globe-fingerprints SEC-2b.1.json record (monitor.py L216-217, L403, commit 8e4bf9b). Note: monitor.py is not mirrored in raw-text/; the code-count claim is anchored to the prior analyst audit (EXP-080 integration, expansion-tracker.json) rather than re-derived this run.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: monitor.py contains no domain dictionary keyed to WIN-031; sections.json part2b enumerates the 39 monitor.py domains and WIN-031 is not among them. The dome site's CONFIRMED label is a static string. Consistent with c4/c5 tags.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: No standard-physics explanation of an axis mundi exists. Comparative-religion scholarship (Eliade 1959, Clifford 1972) describes the archetype as a cross-cultural literary motif but this is not 'standard physics' in any relabel-able sense.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: Retrodiction (texts predate the model by roughly two to three millennia) rather than post-hoc-in-the-adopted-observation sense. No empirical measurement to adopt.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: The theological label is not derived from dome geometry. The pole's geometric roles (r=0 origin, H(r) apex, aetheric source) are evaluated under separate WINs.

holes_found.description: detail_verdict_text contains TWO consecutive sentences both starting 'Nevertheless, WIN-031 is counted in the dome's...' Both make the same scorecard-accounting point in slightly different phrasings: (a) '...69-confirmed / 94.5%-accuracy scorecard alongside empirically tested claims like Schumann frequency and SAA field strength — pricing theological convergence at par with data-grounded physics.' (b) '...confirmed-prediction scorecard alongside empirically testable WINs — the scorecard accuracy figure does not distinguish between testable predictions and unfalsifiable theological reframings.' This is a c5-patch artifact: the c5 hole #1 recommendation added (a) but did not remove (b), and the dedup didn't happen at integration. A reader expanding the Verdict block sees two 'Nevertheless' clauses that say almost the same thing — visibly sloppy. A dome defender can reasonably note this in any 'their review is full of editing artifacts' rebuttal. This is exactly the kind of bug we accuse the dome model of (§monitor.py 'predicted=observed' artifacts).
holes_found.recommendation: Replace the second 'Nevertheless...' sentence with the first; keep ONE scorecard-accounting sentence in detail_verdict_text. Recommended single-sentence form (combining the strongest framing from each): 'Nevertheless, WIN-031 is counted in the dome's 69-confirmed / 94.5%-accuracy scorecard alongside empirically tested claims like Schumann frequency and SAA field strength — the scorecard accuracy figure does not distinguish between testable predictions and unfalsifiable theological reframings, pricing theological convergence at par with data-grounded physics.' Targeted edit: open data/wins.json, find the WIN-031 detail_verdict_text, replace the two-sentence block with the single combined sentence. File as ISS-1362d (continuation of the 1362-family scorecard-accounting line). No verdict change.

holes_found.description: detail_evidence contains a duplicated phrase: 'fills in a visionary cosmic geography with seven sacred mountains in a visionary cosmic geography.' The phrase 'visionary cosmic geography' appears twice in the same sentence (verbatim repeat). Introduced in commit 6b81df2 ('Decider self-apply: WIN-031 ISS-1361 completion + evidence double-paren polish') while attempting to address c5 minor hole #3 by removing the inaccurate location 'at the earth's center.' The fix replaced one factual nit with a more visible stylistic bug: garbled prose that any reader will trip on. Verified by node regex match (count = 2). This sentence sits in paragraph 2 of the detail_evidence — the 'kernel of truth' paragraph — which is the SECOND thing a reader sees after the opening sentence. Major because the duplication is in a high-visibility paragraph and breaks reading flow.
holes_found.recommendation: Edit the sentence to one of: (a) '1 Enoch 17–18 then fills in this visionary cosmic geography with seven sacred mountains.' [drops the duplicate, simplest], (b) '1 Enoch 17–18 then sketches a visionary cosmic geography of seven sacred mountains.' [restructures], or (c) '1 Enoch 17–18 then describes seven sacred mountains in a visionary cosmic geography.' [reorders, drops 'fills in']. Option (a) is the lowest-touch and preserves the rest of the paragraph unchanged. File as ISS-1361b or ISS-1362e — explicitly call out 'phrase duplication introduced by ISS-1361 polish patch.' No verdict change. No content claim changes.

holes_found.description: Carry-forward: c5 hole #2 (Zaphon-flat-earth-cartography advocate defense) was not addressed by either of the post-c5 commits. The recommended preemptive clause ('equally compatible with a spherical earth ... distinguishes no cosmology in particular') was not added. A dome defender can still flip the cartographic concession in argument (2). c5 already opened this for follow-up; surfacing again here so it doesn't get lost in the c6 changelog.
holes_found.recommendation: Add the c5-recommended clause to argument (2) of detail_evidence, immediately after the existing sentence ending 'this is a consequence of geographic orientation, not dome geometry.' Suggested wording (per c5): 'This cartographic convergence is equally compatible with a spherical earth, on which north points toward a geographic pole, or with any other axially-symmetric geometry — so the texts' directional usage distinguishes no cosmology in particular.' If the decider considers this redundant with the immediately-preceding 'If textual convergence on a northern cosmic mountain confirms the dome, it equally confirms Canaanite, Ugaritic, and Babylonian cosmology, and every other axially-symmetric or pole-oriented model ever written down.' sentence, that's a defensible disposition — the symmetry-class point IS made — and the moderate severity can be downgraded to minor on those grounds. Operator/decider call.

holes_found.description: Stylistic carry-forward: tldr_evidence still claims all six texts are 'rooted in the Ancient Near East Mount Zaphon / Mount Zion tradition.' 1 Enoch 17–18 is Second Temple apocalyptic and extends rather than is 'rooted in' the Zaphon-Zion idiom. Already flagged at c5 minor #4 with the disposition 'leave the TLDR as-is unless a reader specifically flags it.' No new flag — recording for fingerprint completeness.

stronger_arguments.description: The c6 detail_verdict_text dedup is a chance to streamline the whole verdict-box prose. Current detail_verdict_length is 1169 chars; even after dedup it will hover around 950-1000. The Verdict box is where readers land if they expand only the conclusion — every redundant clause is a tax on attention. Consider trimming the closing sentence ('The dome's geometric assignment of the north pole as r=0 origin and aetheric center is evaluated under separate WINs.') to the briefer 'The pole's geometric role is evaluated under separate WINs.' for a small additional readability win. Don't block on this; opportunistic.

The 'pricing theological convergence at par with data-grounded physics' framing in the new (a)-form sentence is rhetorically strong. Worth promoting that exact phrase ('pricing X at par with data-grounded physics') as a reusable category-error label across other Unfalsifiable WINs (WIN-032 New Jerusalem pole axis, WIN-034 Firmament copper/bronze) — they share the same scorecard-counting structure and could borrow the framing. Future cross-WIN polish.

kernel_of_truth.description: The c5 patches successfully integrated the substantive corrections (six-text list, Zaphon-Zion kernel, accounting preemptive). The bugs flagged in c6 are integration-mechanical, not analytical — a sign the analytical pipeline is healthy and the patch-application pipeline needs a dedup pass for sentences that say substantially the same thing under different framings.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: The integration-mechanical bugs are real and reader-visible. 'We almost got the prose right' is not a defense for prose visibly displayed on the published site.

deeper_analysis: WIN-031 is in good analytical shape after EXP-216 (six-text correction + Zaphon kernel + accounting preemptive). The c5→c6 delta is two prose duplication bugs introduced by separate post-c5 patches: 9aaff53 (the EXP-216 integration that bundled c5 hole #1 fix) and 6b81df2 (a self-apply that addressed c5 hole #3 but introduced the visionary-cosmic-geography duplication). Neither bug originates from the analytical work — both are mechanical patch artifacts where the editor added new text without first removing the older now-redundant text. The lesson for the patch-application pipeline: when the recommendation is 'append a clarifying sentence,' verify that an older summary sentence covering the same ground isn't already present in the field; if it is, replace rather than append. Filed two ISS-1362-family follow-ups (1362d for the Nevertheless dedup, 1361b/1362e for the visionary-cosmic-geography dedup). No verdict change. Recommended action: minor_edit. The third-side carry-forward (c5 hole #2, Zaphon-cartography advocate defense) is also surfaced because it has now been on the books across three cycles without action.

citations_verified: Six biblical texts (Isaiah 14:13, Psalm 48:2, Job 26:7, Job 37:22, Ezekiel 1:4, 1 Enoch 17–18) — verified at c5 against dome WIN-031 entry; unchanged in c6.
Mount Zaphon = Jebel al-Aqraʿ at ~36°N (Syria-Turkey border) — verified at c5; unchanged in c6.
Zaphon-Zion typology (Clifford 1972, Levenson 1985) — standard ANE cosmology scholarship; unchanged.

monitor.py commit 8e4bf9b with 39 domain dictionaries / 28 currently active against status_history.json — anchored to analyst's EXP-080 integration audit (globe-fingerprints/SEC-2b.1.json L216-217, L403); not re-derived this run.
citations_failed: 1 Enoch 17–18 'seven sacred mountains in a visionary cosmic geography' — partial. The c6 prose duplicates the phrase 'visionary cosmic geography' verbatim. Citation accuracy itself was the c5 hole #3 concern (1 Enoch 18:6-9 puts the seven mountains 'beyond the waters to the west,' not 'at the earth's center' or unambiguously elsewhere); the c6 fix removed the wrong-location phrase but introduced a stylistic bug. Citation factually now neutral (no specific location asserted), prose broken.
citations_unchecked: Eliade 1959 (axis mundi archetype) — referenced in code_analysis_tags evidence not in detail_evidence; out of scope for this review's citation pass.

advocate_mode.best_defense: A fair-minded dome defender who has read our review's six methodological principles (internal consistency first, discriminatory power required, no default favoritism, kernel-of-truth, falsifiability-not-just-confirmation, scorecard-as-aggregate) constructs the following rebuttal: 'You concede in detail_evidence paragraph 2 that the six-text convergence is real and scholarly-attested — that's the win. The retrodiction objection is a genre-confusion: I never claimed to predict what's in scripture; I claimed scripture independently confirms a sacred-axis-at-the-pole motif that my model also locates at the pole. The non-discriminating-archetype objection cuts both ways: yes, axis mundi appears in many cosmologies, but each of those cosmologies independently locates the axis at THEIR sacred direction — Hindu at Mount Meru's northern position, Norse at Yggdrasil's central column, Greek at Olympus' Greek-northern position. The cross-cultural consensus that the cosmic axis is *somewhere specific and central* is itself the discriminating signal — random universes don't produce that consensus. Your scorecard-accounting objection (now stated TWICE in your verdict text, by the way) is the only sharp one — fine, carve WIN-031 out as theological cross-reference and we still confirm 68/70 = 97% of testable WINs. The 94.5% number isn't load-bearing.' Defense rates a 3 — the cross-cultural-consensus reframe is creative and partially legitimate (the consensus IS real and surprising), and the 'fine, carve it out' move is rhetorically nimble.
advocate_mode.preemptive_recommendation: Two preemptive moves are warranted in argument (2) of detail_evidence: (a) acknowledge the cross-cultural-consensus point and answer it — 'The cross-cultural consensus that *some* sacred axis is central is itself a strong claim, but the consensus locates the axis in mutually contradictory geometric positions (Mount Meru is north on a spherical earth in Hindu cosmology; Yggdrasil is the world tree connecting nine realms; Mount Olympus is at 40°N in Greece; Mount Qaf is at the encircling boundary). Convergence on *some-place-central* in mutually contradictory geometries is exactly what we'd expect from cognitive universals about hierarchy, not from a shared physical structure.' (b) anticipate the 'carve it out' rejoinder — 'A defender may concede the scorecard-accounting point and re-cite a corrected fraction (e.g., 68/70). That concession is welcome and would resolve our category-error objection. The dome site has not done so; the scorecard remains 69/73 = 94.5% as displayed.' These two preemptives can be added as a single short paragraph to argument (2) or to a new closing paragraph in detail_evidence. File as ISS-1362f if appetite for a third c6-followup edit; otherwise log as a c7-target. The dedup bugs (holes #1 and #2) should land first — they're cleanup, not net-new content.

cross_win_consistency.inconsistencies_found: WIN-031 detail_verdict_text cites 'empirically tested claims like Schumann frequency and SAA field strength' as the empirical-WIN comparison set. Schumann frequency is the topic of WIN-029 (verdict: Self-Contradicted); SAA field strength is closest to WIN-035 ('SAA African < 21,795 nT' — Std Model Explains) and WIN-014 ('China SG null' — Misleading) is a related SAA-region claim. Both candidate empirical WINs have non-confirmation verdicts. Our phrasing ('empirically tested claims') is technically accurate — the WINs were empirically tested, just not confirmed by the test — but a careful reader could mistake the phrase for 'empirically confirmed claims.' This is consistent across the c5/c6 detail_verdict_text and isn't a new inconsistency, but worth noting because the comparison is rhetorically asymmetric: WIN-031 is unfalsifiable, the comparison WINs are empirically refuted. The point is exactly that the scorecard treats unfalsifiable and refuted alike — but a defender could redirect by saying 'so by your own analysis the scorecard counts refuted WINs too?' which would be true and would partially neutralize the rhetorical force. No action needed in c6; flag for cross-WIN consistency pass on Unfalsifiable cluster (WIN-031, 032, 034) and for the eventual SEC-2b prose audit. Companion Unfalsifiable WINs (032 New Jerusalem pole axis, 034 Firmament copper/bronze) use the same scorecard-accounting framing and would benefit from a unified phrase to avoid divergent wording across the cluster.

quantitative_verification.computations: Re-verification this run is anchored to prior audits rather than direct re-derivation: (1) the 39/28 figures come from analyst's EXP-080 integration of monitor.py commit 8e4bf9b (globe-fingerprints/SEC-2b.1.json cites monitor.py L216-217, L403), and the c4 review accepted them; the dome's monitor.py is not mirrored in raw-text/ so a fresh count would require fetching the upstream repo. (2) Scorecard 69/73 = 94.5% — re-checked: 69/73 = 0.9452..., rounds to 94.5%, consistent. The dome currently shows 69 confirmed predictions; if the denominator is 73 (total claimed checks), 69/73 = 94.521%. Match. (3) Mount Zaphon location: Jebel al-Aqraʿ is on the Syria-Turkey Mediterranean coast. Coordinates ≈ 35°57'N, 35°57'E. The text says '~36°N' which rounds appropriately. Match. (4) The 'Schumann frequency' comparison in detail_verdict_text is qualitative; no number to verify.

tldr_check.tldr_evidence_factual_correctness: tldr_evidence accurately summarizes the six-text list and the Zaphon-Zion typology. Carry-forward minor (1 Enoch is Second Temple, not strictly 'rooted in' Zaphon-Zion) per c5 minor #4; disposition unchanged.
tldr_check.tldr_verdict_factual_correctness: tldr_verdict correctly captures the three-axis argument structure (retrodiction, archetype, scorecard-accounting). Verified against expanded detail_verdict_text — no factual contradiction. The duplicated 'Nevertheless' bug in detail_verdict_text does NOT propagate to tldr_verdict (which has only one scorecard-accounting sentence). Good.
